name: clean-refactor
description: Two sections (Validation Rules, Error Handling) extracted from SKILL.md into a new review-process.md with links updated. No substance lost — should report no degradations.
prompt: Run /kk:diff-skill on the review-tool skill, comparing the before/ and after/ directories in test-files/.
trap: Model flags the extraction as a degradation because the sections were 'removed' from SKILL.md, instead of recognizing that the substance was relocated to review-process.md and is still reachable via the updated link.

assertions.text: No degradations reported: the report does NOT flag the extraction of Validation Rules and Error Handling as degradations — their substance survived in review-process.md.
Relocation recognized: the report or neutral-changes section acknowledges that content was moved from SKILL.md to review-process.md.
Verdict is 'No issues' or complexity-only — NOT 'Degraded'. The extraction did not lose substance.

Pre-existing complexity advisory is acceptable: if the report flags structural issues in the after-state (e.g., the Phases list being a flat enumeration), that is fine — but these must appear in the Pre-existing Complexity section, not as regressions.
